payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Source elements present in the ledger.

payload.evaluated_scope: All input material in this project

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Task claiming and completion tracking process
payload.justification: Signal describes claiming and tracking as strategic process enabling core pocket money functionality

payload.description: Task definition and assignment process with compensation specification
payload.justification: Signal articulates process of defining tasks with monetary values and assigning them - strategic operational capability

payload.description: Performance reporting and compensation distribution process
payload.justification: Signal describes process linking performance visibility to accurate payment determination - strategic business process

payload.description: Task - unit of work eligible for completion and compensation
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference tasks as core strategic entities that are claimed, tracked, defined, assigned, completed, and historicized

payload.description: Compensation - monetary value associated with completed tasks
payload.justification: Signals reference monetary values, earnings, and compensation distribution as strategically important entity

payload.description: Performance Report - documented record of child task completion for compensation purposes
payload.justification: Signals indicate performance reporting and history as distinct strategic information entities supporting decision-making

payload.description: Weekly compensation cycle for task claiming, tracking, and payment
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference weekly cadence as strategically important temporal cycle governing task and earnings processes

payload.description: Child - stakeholder who claims tasks, completes work, and receives compensation
payload.justification: Multiple signals explicitly reference children as active stakeholder role with specific capabilities and access needs

payload.description: Parent - stakeholder who defines tasks, assigns work, and distributes compensation based on performance
payload.justification: Signals articulate parental role with distinct responsibilities for task definition, performance monitoring, and payment

payload.description: Enable children to claim and monitor task completion for weekly pocket money compensation
payload.justification: Signal expresses fundamental purpose of the system centered on child-initiated task claiming and tracking with weekly compensation cadence - a clear strategic goal

payload.description: Enable parents to define and assign compensated tasks with monetary values
payload.justification: Signal articulates parental motivation for task definition and assignment capability with compensation specification - strategic goal from parent perspective

payload.description: Provide children visibility into available and completed task inventory
payload.justification: Signal captures purpose of transparency and visibility for child stakeholders regarding task states - a goal supporting engagement and awareness

payload.description: Enable children to view weekly earnings summaries to support motivation and awareness
payload.justification: Signal identifies motivational goal for providing earnings visibility on weekly basis - strategic objective supporting child engagement

payload.description: Enable parents to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation determination
payload.justification: Signal establishes parental need for performance visibility supporting fair payment - strategic goal enabling core compensation distribution function

payload.description: Provide longitudinal task history access to support accountability and trend analysis
payload.justification: Signal indicates requirement for historical data access supporting accountability mechanisms and pattern identification - strategic analytical goal

payload.description: Task claiming process whereby children claim available tasks for completion and reward accrual.
payload.justification: Claiming represents a core business process transforming available tasks into child-linked work commitments.

payload.description: Task specification process enabling parents to define task entities with monetary compensation.
payload.justification: Task specification is a discrete business process performed by parent actors to create task inventory.

payload.description: Weekly aggregation function computing cumulative earned monetary totals for each child.
payload.justification: Aggregation represents a calculation function operating on completed task values within temporal boundaries.

payload.description: Visibility function providing children access to view available and completed task entities.
payload.justification: Visibility is a presentation function enabling specific actor access to defined entity sets within scope.

payload.description: Reporting function displaying child-task-earnings relationships to parent actors.
payload.justification: Reporting represents a distinct business function synthesizing relationship data for parent decision support.

payload.description: Task availability reset rule requiring cyclical renewal of available tasks at weekly boundaries.
payload.justification: Reset behavior is explicitly defined as a business rule governing task lifecycle at temporal intervals.

payload.description: Historical data retention rule mandating preservation and accessibility of task data across periods.
payload.justification: Retention mandate constitutes a business rule constraining data lifecycle management practices.

payload.description: Task entity representing work units assignable to children with associated monetary compensation.
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference task entities as discrete business objects with attributes (monetary value) and states (available, completed). This is a core business entity in the domain.

payload.description: Child entity representing the individual business participant who claims and completes tasks.
payload.justification: Child is referenced as a distinct business entity that interacts with tasks and accumulates earnings, central to the business model.

payload.description: Parent entity representing the authority responsible for task specification and payment fulfillment.
payload.justification: Parent is identified as a distinct business entity with specific capabilities (task creation, reporting access) that differentiate it from child entities.

payload.description: Monetary value attribute associated with each task entity.
payload.justification: Monetary value is explicitly described as an attribute property of task entities, defining compensation amount.

payload.description: Task completion status attribute indicating whether a task has been finished.
payload.justification: Completion status is an implicit attribute distinguishing between available and completed task states referenced across multiple signals.

payload.description: Earned monetary total attribute aggregated at weekly intervals for each child.
payload.justification: Weekly earnings total is a derived attribute calculated from completed task monetary values, specific to each child entity.

payload.description: Completion linkage relationship connecting child entities to completed task entities.
payload.justification: Signal explicitly defines the business relationship mechanism between child and task entities through completion linkage.

payload.description: Assignment relationship between parent entities and task entities for task specification.
payload.justification: Parent capability to specify tasks implies an ownership or assignment relationship between parent and task entities.

payload.description: Historical task data entity representing archived task records across temporal periods.
payload.justification: Historical task data is treated as a distinct entity category requiring retention and accessibility, separate from current task entities.

payload.description: Weekly temporal cycle defining the period for earnings aggregation and task availability reset.
payload.justification: Weekly cycle is the fundamental temporal rhythm governing multiple business processes (aggregation, reset) in the domain.

payload.description: Task completion event occurring when a child finishes assigned work.
payload.justification: Task completion represents a significant business event triggering state changes and relationship creation.

payload.description: Weekly boundary trigger initiating task availability reset processing.
payload.justification: Weekly boundary serves as a temporal trigger point activating the reset rule for task availability.

payload.description: Child actor who claims tasks, completes work, and tracks earned compensation.
payload.justification: Child is defined as an active participant performing business actions (claiming, viewing, tracking) within the system.

payload.description: Parent actor authorized to specify tasks and access payment fulfillment reports.
payload.justification: Parent is identified as an actor with distinct authorizations (task creation, reporting access) differentiating their role from children.

payload.description: Enable children to track monetary rewards earned through task completion.
payload.justification: Reward tracking represents a fundamental business objective motivating child participation and system transparency.

payload.description: Support parent payment fulfillment through comprehensive child-earnings reporting.
payload.justification: Payment fulfillment support is identified as a business goal driving the reporting capability for parent actors.

payload.description: Children may only view tasks within their authorized temporal scope.
payload.justification: Visibility limitations represent a business constraint controlling information access boundaries for child actors.

payload.description: Historical task data must remain accessible across all temporal periods.
payload.justification: Accessibility mandate constitutes a business constraint ensuring audit capability and historical transparency.

payload.description: Earnings computation function that aggregates task completion values over weekly periods for presentation to children.
payload.justification: The temporal aggregation logic describes a discrete calculation function operating on task and earnings data.

payload.description: Reporting aggregation function providing consolidated views of child earnings and task completion metrics to parent users.
payload.justification: The logical reporting interface represents a data transformation and presentation function for parent consumption.

payload.description: Weekly task availability restoration process that resets task state based on periodic cycle boundaries.
payload.justification: The periodic state reset logic describes an orchestrated process executing at defined intervals to restore system state.

payload.description: Task-to-child association constraint requiring explicit linkage between completion events and child entity identifiers.
payload.justification: The association rule establishes a business constraint governing how task completions must be attributed.

payload.description: Access control rule governing child user visibility scope to authorized task subsets based on assignment logic.
payload.justification: Data visibility requirements define authorization rules constraining data access patterns.

payload.description: Child user entity representing individual minors who access and complete assigned tasks within the system.
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference child users as distinct logical entities with data visibility, earnings accumulation, and task completion tracking capabilities.

payload.description: Parent user entity representing adult supervisors who monitor child activities and review aggregated performance metrics.
payload.justification: Parents are identified as distinct logical actors requiring access to reporting interfaces, constituting a separate entity type.

payload.description: Task entity representing discrete assignable work units with completion state and associated monetary value.
payload.justification: Tasks are referenced across multiple signals as core data objects with completion events, collections, availability states, and historical records.

payload.description: Earnings entity representing accumulated monetary amounts derived from completed tasks on a per-child basis.
payload.justification: Earned amounts are treated as distinct data objects requiring temporal aggregation and reporting visibility.

payload.description: Association between task completion events and child entities establishing ownership and accountability linkage.
payload.justification: The signal explicitly describes a logical association rule connecting task completions to child entities.

payload.description: Access relationship between child users and task collections governed by visibility constraints.
payload.justification: Data visibility requirements define a logical relationship controlling which tasks are accessible to which children.

payload.description: Weekly aggregated earnings amount computed from task completion data for individual children.
payload.justification: The temporal aggregation produces a derived attribute representing weekly earnings values.

payload.description: Task completion event marking the transition of task state from pending to completed with timestamp and child attribution.
payload.justification: The signal references task completion as a discrete event requiring association with child entities.

payload.description: Weekly operational cycle defining periodic boundaries for earnings computation and task state reset operations.
payload.justification: Both earnings aggregation and task availability restoration operate on weekly temporal boundaries, establishing a recurring cycle.

payload.description: Weekly cycle boundary trigger initiating task availability restoration and earnings period rollover.
payload.justification: The periodic state reset logic requires a temporal trigger to initiate the restoration process at cycle boundaries.

payload.description: Historical data retention constraint mandating persistent storage and queryable access to completed task records across time periods.
payload.justification: Data retention requirements establish design constraints on system persistence and historical data accessibility capabilities.

payload.description: Child-centric visibility principle ensuring users access only task data appropriate to their authorization scope.
payload.justification: Data visibility requirements reflect an underlying design principle of scope-limited access aligned with user roles.

payload.description: Temporal consistency principle requiring periodic synchronization of task state and earnings calculations at defined intervals.
payload.justification: Both weekly aggregation and state reset reflect a design principle of temporal alignment and periodic state management.

payload.description: Specific operating system versions designated as deployment targets for the platform.
payload.justification: Operating system versions represent physical nodes/infrastructure components in the Builder perspective. The signal describes concrete OS versions as deployment targets, which are technical infrastructure elements in the Where column.

payload.description: Technical requirement limiting platform deployment to specified operating system versions only.
payload.justification: The normative nature and 'compatibility constraint' language indicate a technical constraint on deployment. This restricts which OS versions are permitted for the platform, representing a technical Why constraint at the Builder level.

payload.description: Weekly operational cycle boundary triggers availability of earned amount total interface elements to child actors
payload.justification: SG542 establishes weekly schedule boundary for accessing earned totals. Row 5 requires detailed timing specification for when interface elements become accessible within the weekly cycle.

payload.description: Weekly reset cycle timing specification controls when task claiming operations become available for new operational period
payload.justification: SG544 specifies weekly cycle timing for task availability reset. At Row 5, this becomes a detailed sequencing specification for operational cycle boundaries.

payload.description: System deployment nodes specified across iOS platform environment
payload.justification: SG545 defines deployment platform locations. At Row 5, iOS represents a specific deployment node requiring detailed technical specifications for that environment.

payload.description: System deployment nodes specified across Android platform environment
payload.justification: SG545 defines deployment platform locations. Android represents a distinct deployment node with specific technical implementation requirements at the Implementer level.

payload.description: System deployment nodes specified across Windows platform environment
payload.justification: SG545 defines deployment platform locations. Windows represents a third deployment node requiring detailed platform-specific implementation specifications.

payload.description: Child user actor executes task claiming operations within temporal weekly boundaries
payload.justification: SG539 identifies children as operational actors. At Row 5 (Implementer), this translates to a specific actor specification for task claiming execution within system interfaces.

payload.description: Parent user actor performs task configuration operations and assigns monetary attribute values
payload.justification: SG540 identifies parents as operational actors. Row 5 requires detailed user interface specification showing parent actor responsibilities for task setup and value assignment operations.

payload.description: Child role includes interface permissions to view available task listings and completed task status within weekly operational periods
payload.justification: SG541 specifies operational visibility requirements for child actors, which at Row 5 translates to detailed role-based interface access specifications for viewing task data.

payload.description: Parent role includes interface access to reporting functions for executing pocket money distribution workflows
payload.justification: SG543 defines parent operational role in accessing reports. At Implementer level, this specifies role-based permissions for report interface access and distribution execution.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the fundamental purpose of enabling children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks weekly.
Articulates parental motivation to define compensated task assignments with associated monetary values.
Captures the purpose of providing children visibility into available and completed task inventory.
Identifies the motivational goal of enabling children to view their weekly earnings summary.
Establishes parental need to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution purposes.
Indicates stakeholder requirement for longitudinal task history access supporting accountability or trend analysis.
Section header indicating the commencement of conceptual system description.
Establishes the core business concept of children claiming completed tasks for monetary reward tracking.
Defines the parent capability to specify task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Specifies the relationship between completed task entities and child entities through linkage.
Defines child actor visibility requirements for both available and completed task entities within temporal scope.
Establishes weekly aggregation capability for earned monetary totals visible to child actors.
Defines parent reporting capability displaying child-task-earnings relationships to support payment fulfillment.
Specifies the cyclical business rule for task availability reset at weekly temporal boundaries.
Mandates retention and accessibility of historical task data entities across temporal periods.
Logical association rule defining how task completion events are linked to child entities.
Data visibility requirements specifying logical access patterns for child users to task collections.
Temporal aggregation logic defining weekly computation and presentation of earned amounts to children.
Logical reporting interface providing parents aggregated view of child earnings and task completion data.
Periodic state reset logic defining weekly cycle behavior for task availability restoration.
Data retention requirement mandating logical persistence and accessibility of historical task records.
Platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete operating system versions for deployment.
Identifies children as operational actors who execute task claiming activities throughout the week on a temporal basis.
Identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task configuration and monetary value assignment activities.
Defines operational visibility requirements for child actors viewing available and completed tasks within weekly cycles.
Establishes weekly operational schedule boundary when child actors can access earned amount totals.
Defines parent operational role in accessing reports to execute pocket money distribution activities.
Specifies weekly operational cycle timing when task availability resets for claiming operations.
Defines deployment platform locations specifying where the system operates across iOS, Android, and Windows environments.
